acceptance_criteria: No paper, site page, widget caption, or README line labels an unproved theorem as proved.
Every proved theorem id resolves to a Lean declaration and passes fake-proof checks.
Every planned, exploratory, blocked, or deferred theorem remains labeled that way in prose.
targets.guardrail: Do not edit theorem meanings merely to make prose cleaner.
targets.next_action: Claim-language audit added and wired into make check; rerun after each proof, paper, README, or Living Book status update.
targets.objective: Ensure every reader-facing theorem claim has an honest status and every proved claim resolves to compiled Lean.

acceptance_criteria: Each paper has a clear aim, theorem spine, proved core, sidecar pointer, and guardrail.
Formal claims either cite theorem ids or are labeled background, exploratory, planned, blocked, deferred, or future.
Speculative application language is separated from proved theorem language.
targets.guardrail: Cleaner prose must not upgrade proof status.
targets.next_action: Source-trail coverage now exists for all 35 manifest papers; continue the paper-quality sweep with sentence-level guardrail clarity and theorem-language precision.
targets.objective: Make every paper easier to read while preserving exact proof status and theorem ids.

acceptance_criteria: Recurring project terms have dictionary ids.
Dictionary forbidden meanings match paper and site guardrails.
No page or paper invents a parallel term when a dictionary term already exists.
targets.guardrail: Do not duplicate dictionary definitions manually in site prose when generated boxes can carry them.
targets.next_action: Root and dimension dictionaries now share one validator; continue scanning Phase IV/V vocabulary for missing terms before the next prose sweep.
targets.objective: Keep project vocabulary stable across papers, manifests, Python, Lean, and Living Book pages.

acceptance_criteria: Every paper sidecar path in the paper manifest exists.
Python sidecars test the examples named in their papers.
Benchmark sidecars include ordinary baselines and guardrails before usefulness claims.
targets.guardrail: Do not call Python tests formal proofs.
targets.next_action: Review sidecar names and benchmark fixtures for stale examples or missing negative controls.
targets.objective: Ensure Python examples and Lean sidecars match paper claims without being presented as proofs.

acceptance_criteria: The reader path, roadmap, target index, theorem index, dictionary index, and application pages render.
Widgets are labeled as intuition or executable reference, not proof.
Theorem and dictionary boxes resolve from generated data.
targets.guardrail: Do not make scaffolded higher-dimensional pages look complete.
targets.next_action: Run browser checks on the reader path and S1 widget pages after widget or navigation changes.
targets.objective: Keep the Living Book navigable, mobile-friendly, generated-data linked, and proof-status honest.

acceptance_criteria: README matches the actual proof, paper, Living Book, license, and GitHub Pages state.
Peripheral docs point to source-of-truth manifests instead of duplicating drifting status tables.
No transient reports, caches, or local-only artifacts are tracked.
targets.area: Public repo onboarding and peripheral docs
targets.guardrail: Do not add new peripheral docs unless they replace a real hidden-context gap.
targets.next_action: Review README, GitHub Pages docs, and license/onboarding text after the next major roadmap update.
targets.objective: Make the public repository easy to understand, verify, cite, and navigate.

acceptance_criteria: Every exploratory benchmark has a baseline or explicitly records that the baseline is future work.
AI, compute, data, rendering, and orientation claims do not imply universal improvement.
MLX/Mac-first local wording remains distinct from CUDA or external portability notes.
targets.guardrail: Benchmark results are measurements or fixtures, not theorem proofs.
targets.next_action: Audit application papers after each new benchmark fixture or baseline comparison.
targets.objective: Ensure application claims remain benchmarked, baseline-aware, and separated from mathematical proofs.

acceptance_criteria: Paper, site, manifest, and dictionary links resolve.
Generated data tracked by policy is current.
Rendered outputs, caches, and local install artifacts remain ignored unless intentionally tracked.
targets.area: Final link, source, and clutter sweep
targets.guardrail: Do not delete user work or unrelated untracked files during cleanup.
targets.next_action: Perform a link and clutter audit before any release-style public announcement.
targets.objective: Remove stale links, dead source references, and tracked clutter while preserving durable generated data.
